**1. Reverse chart order (index.html)** — trivial, doing now. Site currently renders `data.models` as-is; `gen_data.py` sorts ascending. I'll reverse in the frontend (matches your pin) and fix the subtitle.

**2. Auto leaderboard in README** — approach: have `gen_data.py` also emit `assets/leaderboard.svg` (dependency-free, hand-rolled SVG matching the dark theme; GitHub renders inline SVG in README). Then a GitHub Actions workflow regenerates + commits it whenever eval files / config change. No external service, no matplotlib, diffs are readable.

Implementing both.
Now the SVG generator in `gen_data.py` + Makefile target + workflow + README embed.

transcript.name: write
transcript.content:
name: update-leaderboard

# Regenerates data.json + assets/leaderboard.svg from the committed
# benchmark/eval/*.json results and commits them back so the README
# chart stays in sync without manual image editing.

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - benchmark/eval/**
      - benchmark/questions.json
      - config.yml
      - gen_data.py
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  rebuild:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.x"

      - name: Regenerate data.json + leaderboard.svg
        run: python3 gen_data.py

      - name: Commit changes
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add data.json assets/leaderboard.svg
          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
            echo "no changes"
          else
            git commit -m "chore: regenerate leaderboard [skip ci]"
            git push
          fi

transcript.text: Done. **index.html** — chart now renders highest→lowest (`[...data.models].reverse()`, no mutation of data) + subtitle fixed to "highest to lowest". **README leaderboard** — `gen_data.py` now also writes `assets/leaderboard.svg` (dependency-free, dark-themed, sorted desc, committed in repo). README embeds it via `![leaderboard](assets/leaderboard.svg)`. GitHub renders inline SVGs from the repo. **Automation** — `.github/workflows/update-leaderboard.yml` runs `gen_data.py` on push to `main` when eval files / config / generator change, commits `data.json` + `assets/leaderboard.svg` back. Also `workflow_dispatch` for manual trigger. `make chart` alias added. Notes: - Logos deliberately omitted from SVG: external refs are blocked when an SVG loads as `` in GitHub. Colored bars + names + % only. - `gen_data.py` keeps `data.json` ascending (site reverses client-side), so no double-reverse risk. - Workflow uses default `GITHUB_TOKEN` with `contents: write` — no PAT needed. Verify locally: `python3 gen_data.py` already ran clean; SVG at `assets/leaderboard.svg` (4080 bytes).

- are you sure we can't include logos in the leaderboard?
- limit leaderboard.svg to 5 top models
- replace title with just 'catbotbench', increase text size a bit, make it to spice things up a little
- add a couple vertical tiles for the chart (e.g. at 0 20 40 60 80 100)

Good catch — I was wrong about logos. SVG-as-`` is in "secure static mode": external URLs blocked, but **`data:` URIs are allowed**. So base64-embedded logos render on GitHub. With only 5 models the bloat is fine (~60KB). Rewriting `write_svg`:

transcript.text: Let me preview-render to verify layout.

All checks pass: 5 logos embedded (base64 data URIs), 6 gridlines (0/20/40/60/80/100), 5 model rows, title "catbotbench" at size 30.

Done. Summary of changes to `gen_data.py` → `write_svg`:

- **Logos**: yes, possible. Embedded as `data:image/png;base64,...` in `<image>` — renders under SVG secure-static mode on GitHub (external URLs blocked, `data:` URIs allowed). ~113KB total, fine.
- **Top 5 only**: `sorted(...)[:5]`.
- **Title**: just `catbotbench`, font-size 30, weight 800, accent→orange linear-gradient fill, plus a 120px accent rule + small `% correct · top 5 · highest to lowest` subline.
- **Vertical tiles**: dashed gridlines at 0/20/40/60/80/100 spanning the chart band, with faint % tick labels above.

Also bumped row height (44px) and bar padding since only 5 rows now.
